name: Esther's Purses
recipeIngredient: 3 Eggs or ¾ cup egg substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the fillings and set aside until ready to use. Heat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat the eggs (or substitute), adding the sugar as you beat. Continue beating, adding the oil and orange juice.
3. In another bowl, combine the dry ingredients, mixing well. Add these to the beaten egg mixture. The dough will be slightly sticky; do not add more flour at this point or the cookies will become hard.
4. On a lightly floured pastry board, roll part of the dough to a thickness of 1/8". Cut out circles with a 3-inch cookie cutter (9 cm). Always include scraps from one rolling with fresh dough from the bowl; this ensures minimal flour use and tender cookies.
5. Fill each cookie circle with about 1 teaspoon of filling (either Date Orange or Nut filling; separate recipes). Shape cookies by folding the dough over the filling. Close the edges with a fork. Repeat with remaining dough.
6. Bake for about 20 minutes, then remove cookies from the oven and cool on a wire rack. While still warm, roll them in powdered sugar. Cool thoroughly before serving. Store in an air-tight container. May be frozen. Makes 6 dozen cookies.
